Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: AI reshapes IT talent's pyramid model
Article Text:
Representative image

.

BENGALURU: The IT industry is undergoing one of its deepest structural shifts in decades as AI reshapes talent needs across the delivery chain. The traditional pyramid model with large teams of junior coders supervised by layers of managers-is giving way to a capability-dense, AI-augmented structure."The shift is from coding depth to problem framing," said Gilroy Mathew, COO of UST. Instead of testing whether candidates can write a Java function, firms now ask whether they can define business problems, break them into AI-executable steps, and validate outputs for bias, risk and completeness.Access to AI tools is narrowing the gap between freshers and mid-level engineers. A well-trained entry-level hire is expected to become productive faster than before.LTIMindtree describes the emerging structure as a "diamond" rather than a pyramid. At the base is a smaller layer of AI-fluent engineers, supported by automation and AI agents handling routine execution. In the middle, architects and managers are evolving into orchestrators-designing AI-first workflows, integrating systems and aligning delivery with business goals."It's not about fewer people; it's about fewer people doing low-judgement work," said Gururaj Deshpande, chief delivery officer at LTIMindtree, arguing that AI-led productivity gains will help clear larger project backlogs rather than simply reduce headcount.One clear shift is the shrinking space for purely supervisory managers. In an AI-led environment, managers are expected to redesign processes around automation, understand agent-based workflows, ensure AI governance and tie execution to business KPIs.Hiring models are changing in parallel. At UST, screening moved from "employability" to "adaptability". Candidates are given real-world business scenarios and evaluated on how they frame problems, use AI tools, validate outputs and factor in ethical considerations.
Article Summary: The IT industry is undergoing one of its deepest structural shifts in decades. The traditional pyramid model with large teams of junior coders is giving way to a capability-dense, AI-augmented structure. A well-trained entry-level hire is expected to become productive faster than before.

Article Title: Buying luxury property in Dubai just got easier: How buyers can win big in market of high-end homes
Article Text:
Dubai’s Luxury Property Market Is Crowded: Here’s How Smart Buyers Are Still Winning Big

Ground decisions in real market data: Not just asking prices



Work with specialised luxury agents, not generalists



Too Many Mansions, Too Little Time? How Bayut Is Helping Luxury Buyers Cut Through the Noise

Prioritise verified and actively available listings



Use valuation and market performance tools as part of your strategy



Maintain visibility across current and future opportunities



Million-Dirham Decisions Made Simple: The Tool Luxury Buyers in Dubai Are Quietly Using

(Disclaimer: Recommendations and views on the stock market, other asset classes or personal finance management tips given by experts are their own. These opinions do not represent the views of The Times of India)





Dubai’s ultra-luxury property segment, including homes priced above Dh20 million, remains one of the hottest real estate markets globally, drawing affluent local and international investors alike. However, while listing volumes are high, identifying the right inventory with real market value and credibility can still be challenging.With hundreds of luxury transactions recorded annually, including more than 750 properties above Dh50m and over 250 valued above Dh100m in 2025 alone, the challenge for affluent buyers is not just seeing inventory but identifying the right opportunities backed by real market behaviour. Platforms like Bayut are simplifying this process with data-led tools, verified listings and specialist agent networks designed to empower discerning buyers.Instead of simply providing endless listings, Bayut focuses on three core pillars of data insights, verified availability and expert partnerships to help buyers separate signal from noise in a dynamic market environment.One of the biggest challenges for luxury buyers is knowing whether a listed price reflects actual market activity rather than wishful thinking.Bayut tackles this by offering transaction-backed insights, drawing on official Dubai Land Department (DLD) records, so that buyers can see what similar properties are really achieving in terms of sales volume and pricing.In 2025, Dubai recorded thousands of high-value property sales, including more than 750 transactions for properties above Dh50 million and over 250 above Dh100 million, underscoring both demand and the need for accurate context. This type of benchmark pricing helps buyers understand if a property is competitively priced, overpriced, or a rare value play — a crucial step before making multi-million-dirham commitments.Luxury real estate is not just about price tags, it is about deep market knowledge, negotiation experience and access to exclusive opportunities. Bayut’s ecosystem highlights specialist agents with proven track records in high-value transactions, rather than generalists who may be active across all segments.Programs like TruBroker, Bayut’s quality standard for agent credibility, help buyers identify intermediaries who are consistent, data-savvy and focused on premium markets. This makes it easier to form partnerships with professionals who understand how to position offers, anticipate buyer preferences and secure coveted properties.Luxury inventory, especially at the very top end, can be distorted by outdated listings, duplicate posts or units that are no longer available. Bayut’s technology emphasises verified listings through tools like TruCheck and cross-referencing with systems like DLD’s Madmoun to ensure that a property you’re considering is truly active and not a relic of old marketing efforts.This verification helps buyers avoid wasted time and misaligned expectations, accelerating decision-making and reducing friction in negotiations.Luxury buyers also benefit from tools that help them evaluate whether asking prices make sense relative to market benchmarks. Bayut’s TruEstimate offers valuation estimates, historical price movement analysis and demand indicators that serve as a sense check for where a property stands, helping buyers discern possible overpricing or hidden opportunity without removing the emotional appeal of design or location.Such analytical signals are especially valuable in a segment where architectural uniqueness or waterfront views can command premium pricing that may not be immediately intuitive.High-end property purchases often emerge from long-term observation rather than last-minute decisions. Features like portfolio tracking (where buyers can keep an eye on pricing trends, status shifts and comparisons across multiple shortlisted properties), give investors a structured way to monitor the market over time, rather than making impulsive choices.This kind of forward-looking visibility keeps serious buyers informed about pricing shifts, new inventory that enters the high-end space, and when timing might be right to make a move. As Dubai continues to attract wealthy buyers from Europe, Asia and the Gulf with significant activity in places like Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Hills Estate and Mohammed Bin Rashid City, the need for clarity and confidence in property selection has never been greater.Bayut’s combination of transaction-backed data, verification, specialist networks and valuation insights positions it not just as a listing portal but as a trusted guide through one of the world’s most dynamic luxury markets. In an era where information overload can mislead as easily as it informs, access to accurate data, specialised expertise and transparent tools helps buyers make smarter and more efficient decisions, whether they are picking a trophy asset or a strategic long-term investment.
Article Summary: Dubai’s ultra-luxury property segment, including homes priced above Dh20 million, remains one of the hottest real estate markets globally. Bayut focuses on three core pillars of data insights, verified availability and expert partnerships to help buyers separate signal from noise.

Article Title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei gets summoned from Pentagon for 'not a friendly meeting'
Article Text:
Representative Image

"Anthropic knows this is not a get-to-know-you meeting. This is not a friendly meeting. This is a sh*t-or-get-off-the-pot meeting".

"having productive conversations, in good faith."

,





What Anthropic said about the Pentagon meeting with Pete Hegseth



"is committed to using frontier AI in support of US national security,"

"all lawful uses".

"

supply chain risk

,"

"The problem with Dario is, with him, it's ideological. We know who we're dealing with,"

Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has reportedly been summoned to the Pentagon by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth for a meeting. Sources claim that this meeting will be anything but routine. In a statement to Axios, a senior official from the US Department of Defence said,The meeting centres on terms for military use of Anthropic's Claude, the only AI model currently available in the military's classified systems and the one used for sensitive defence and intelligence work. The Pentagon, while unwilling to lose access to Claude, is said to be furious with Anthropic for refusing to lift its safeguards fully.The two sides appear to be heading into the meeting at an impasse, with an Anthropic spokesperson saying the company isMeanwhileUS Department of Defence officials say negotiations have shown no progress and are on the verge of breaking down, the Axios report noted.Anthropic has said it is willing to loosen its usage restrictions but wants to draw a firm line around two areas: the mass surveillance of Americans and the development of weapons that fire without human involvement.The companya spokesperson said. However, the Pentagon has pushed back against having to clear individual uses with the company and has demanded that all AI labs make their models available forThe dispute has escalated to the point where the Pentagon has threatened to declare Anthropic awhich would not only void its contracts but also force other companies working with the Pentagon to certify they are not using Claude in those workflows.Defence officials are also discussing other potential tools to pressure Anthropic, with a Defence official saying Hegseth would effectively be presenting Amodei with an ultimatum at the upcoming meeting. Replacing Anthropic would be a considerable task, given how deeply entrenched it is in military systems and the fact that other AI labs currently have less capable models.Amodei has been vocal about the risks of AI and has positioned Anthropic as a safety-focused company. Officials have described a culture clash between Hegseth's Pentagon and the Silicon Valley firm.a senior Pentagon official said. Anthropic is not alone in its concerns, as broader questions remain about whether US law has kept pace with AI's potential to expand surveillance capabilities and about the risks of entrusting AI to power weapons systems.The feud between the Pentagon and Anthropic was further strained after Claude was used in the raid on Venezuela’s ex-president, Nicolas Maduro, in January. Leading the meeting from the Pentagon side will be Hegseth, Deputy Secretary Steve Feinberg and Under Secretary for Research and Engineering Emil Michael, who has been heading negotiations with Anthropic and three other AI model-makers. However, Anthropic declined to name its delegation.
Article Summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has reportedly been summoned to the Pentagon by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth for a meeting. Sources claim that this meeting will be anything but routine. Meeting centres on terms for military use of Anthropic's Claude, the only AI model currently available in the military's classified systems.

Article Title: BLS International introduces AI-powered VoiceBot service to support these visa applicants
Article Text: BLS International has launched an AI-powered VoiceBot for the Spain–Ireland visa mission to provide 24/7 assistance to applicants. As announced by the company, the AI-powered VoiceBot will operate 24x7, providing real-time, human-like interaction and delivering instant, accurate responses to customer queries. With the launch, the company aims to address visa-related enquiries, from basic FAQs to detailed guidance on documentation and consular services. Furthermore, the VoiceBot will support 15+ languages such as Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese, French, Chinese, and Russian, enabling seamless engagement with customers across Europe, the US, Canada, South America, the Middle East, Southeast Asia, China, the RCIS region, and India, with language availability aligned to regional requirements and customer needs.The launch follows a successful deployment at IVAC Canada last year, the VoiceBot service is now being launched for the Spain mission in Ireland, with plans for a subsequent global rollout.Shikhar Aggarwal, Joint Managing Director at BLS International, said, “We are pleased to announce the launch of our AI-powered VoiceBot service, designed to enhance customer experience while establishing new standards for operational excellence.This launch represents a pivotal milestone in our digital transformation strategy, demonstrating our commitment to embedding AI and advanced technologies across our operations. As we continue to scale intelligent solutions, this VoiceBot underscores our strategic focus on delivering seamless, customer-centric innovations that drive sustainable value and define the future of our customer engagement.”Commenting on the VoiceBot launch, Nitin Mishra, Vice President - Global Contact Centres, BLS International, said, “Implementing the VoiceBot has been a testament to our cross-functional capabilities and technical readiness. From architecting the AI framework to ensuring seamless integration with our existing systems, our teams have built a scalable, secure solution that can handle high volumes while maintaining service quality. This deployment establishes a replicable model for rolling out AI-powered solutions across our business, accelerating our time-to-market for future innovations.
Article Summary: BLS International has launched an AI-powered VoiceBot for the Spain–Ireland visa mission to provide 24/7 assistance to applicants. The VoiceBot will support 15+ languages such as Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese, French, Chinese, and Russian.

Article Title: Amazon says Russian-speaking hacker used AI to hit one of world's most deployed network firewalls in 5 weeks
Article Text:
Representative Image

“This investigation highlights how commercial AI services can lower the technical barrier to entry for offensive cyber capabilities. The threat actor in this campaign is not known to be associated with any advanced persistent threat group with state-sponsored resources. They are likely a financially motivated individual or small group who, through AI augmentation, achieved an operational scale that would have previously required a significantly larger and more skilled team,”

“Yet, based on our analysis of public sources, they successfully compromised multiple organizations’ Active Directory environments, extracted complete credential databases, and targeted backup infrastructure, a potential precursor to

ransomware

deployment. Notably, when this actor encountered hardened environments or more sophisticated defensive measures, they simply moved on to softer targets rather than persisting, underscoring that their advantage lies in AI-augmented efficiency and scale, not in deeper technical skill,”

How AI powered the attack



Attack planning:

Code generation:

Operational assistance:

Amazon Threat Intelligence has revealed that a single “unsophisticated” attacker has compromised more than 600 organisations across 55 countries in just over a month. According to the company's findings, the campaign ran from January 11 to February 18, 2026, and targeted FortiGate firewalls – some of the most common security devices used by businesses worldwide. The hacker didn’t use any complex “zero-day” exploits or secret software flaws. Instead, they used commercial AI tools to automate the “grunt work” of cybercrime.the company said.it added. Amazon researchers described the operation as an “AI-powered assembly line”. While the hacker appeared to have limited technical skills, they used multiple commercial AI services (including tools like Claude and DeepSeek) to act as their “staff.”According to the investigation, the AI was used for:Generating step-by-step instructions for moving through a victim’s network.Writing custom scripts in Python and Go to steal passwords and map out internal systems.Scaling well-known attack techniques so that one person could do the work of a whole team.
Article Summary: A single “unsophisticated” attacker has compromised more than 600 organisations across 55 countries in just over a month. Amazon researchers described the operation as an “AI-powered assembly line’ The hacker appeared to have limited technical skills, but used multiple commercial AI services to act as their “staff”

Article Title: The Times of India-Gadgets Now Awards 2025: Winners of Best audio products and Best TVs of the year
Article Text:
The Times of India-Gadgets Now Awards 2026 celebrated top audio and TV innovations. JBL Tour Pro 3 and Apple AirPods Pro 3 led TWS, while Sony WH-1000XM6 dominated headphones. Samsung's Neo QLED QN90F impressed in premium TVs, and Xiaomi's QLED TV FX Pro offered exceptional value in the affordable segment.

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best TWS of the year

JBL Tour Pro 3: Editor's Choice

Apple AirPods Pro 3: People's Choice

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best affordable premium TWS of the year

Noise Master Buds: Editor's Choice

OnePlus Buds 4: People's Choice

OnePlus Nord Buds 3r: Editor's Choice and People's Choice

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best headphones of the year

Sony WH-1000XM6: Editor's Choice & People's Choice

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best portable speaker of the year

Philips S1209: Editor's Choice & People's Choice

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best home audio of the year

Sony Bravia Theatre System 6: Editor's Choice & People's Choice

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best TV of the year

Samsung Neo QLED QN90F: Editor's Choice & People's Choice

Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best affordable TV of the year

Xiaomi QLED TV FX Pro: Editor's Choice & People's Choice

The Times of India-Gadgets Now Awards 2026 have wrapped up, and we have our winners across all the audio and TV categories. This year's crop is interesting—TWS earbuds now double as fitness trackers and real-time translators, headphones have pushed noise cancellation to a point where silence feels like a feature you paid extra for, and Mini LED TVs are giving OLEDs a genuine fight in picture quality. On the audio side, we saw Indian brands like Noise hold their own against global heavyweights, while in the TV space, Xiaomi continued to make affordable QLED panels that punch well above their price. Here's a look at every winner, and what makes each one worth paying attention to.JBL Tour Pro 3 won the Editor's Choice award at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026, while the Apple AirPods Pro 3 was the People's Choice winner.The JBL Tour Pro 3 doesn't just play music—it basically runs a small command centre from inside your pocket. The 1.57-inch touchscreen on the Smart Charging Case 2.0 (30% bigger than its predecessor) lets you skip tracks, tweak ANC levels, and check caller ID without touching your phone—and plug the case into a plane's seatback screen or gym TV via AUX or USB-C, and it turns into a wireless audio transmitter. JBL went with a hybrid dual-driver setup—a balanced armature for the highs and an 11mm dynamic driver for bass—paired with LDAC support, JBL Spatial 360 with head tracking, and True Adaptive Noise Cancellation 2.0 that samples your environment over 50,000 times per second.Add full Auracast support, Bluetooth 5.4, IP55 rating, and 8+24 hours of battery life with ANC on, and nothing else in the TWS market packs this much into one package at around $300.Apple played it smart with the AirPods Pro 3—on the outside, they look almost identical to the Pro 2, but inside, nearly everything has changed. The noise cancellation is now twice as good as the previous generation (four times better than the original AirPods Pro), and the real curveball is an infrared heart rate sensor tucked into each bud that tracks your pulse during 50 different workout types without needing an Apple Watch. Then there's Live Translation, which uses Apple Intelligence to translate conversations in real time between English, French, German, Portuguese, and Spanish—right in your ear. Fit has been improved with foam-infused ear tips in five sizes (including a new XXS option), the IP57 rating means these can handle genuine water immersion, battery life holds at 8 hours with ANC, and the U2 chip in the case enables longer-range Precision Finding—all at $249, the same price Apple has charged since the original AirPods Pro launched in 2019.Noise Master Buds won the Editor's Choice award at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026, while OnePlus Buds 4 was the People's Choice winner.Indian audio brand Noise has been steadily levelling up, and the Master Buds are the clearest proof yet. These sit in that sweet spot where you're getting flagship-tier features—strong active noise cancellation, a well-tuned sound profile, and reliable call quality—without the flagship price. The build quality feels premium in the hand, the app gives you enough control to fine-tune the experience, and for anyone who wants a capable pair of daily-driver earbuds without spending north of Rs 5,000, the Noise Master Buds make a very convincing case.OnePlus has always understood the assignment when it comes to value, and the Buds 4 are no exception. The ANC punches above its price bracket, the sound is warm and detailed enough for most genres, and the integration with OnePlus phones is seamless. These won't out-spec a JBL Tour Pro 3 or AirPods Pro 3, but they were never trying to—they do what they do well, and they do it at a price that makes you think twice about spending more.Winners of GN Awards 2026: Best affordable TWS of the yearOnePlus Nord Buds 3r won both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice awards at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026.When a product sweeps both awards, it usually means one thing—it's the obvious pick at its price. OnePlus has crammed in a surprisingly clean audio experience, decent passive isolation, and a comfortable fit into a pair of buds that costs about as much as a couple of movie tickets. There's no unnecessary bloat here—just solid bass, clear vocals, and enough battery life to get through a long commute and then some. For budget TWS buyers, this was the one to beat in 2025, and nothing really did.Sony WH-1000XM6 won both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice awards at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026.Sony's XM series has dominated the premium headphone space for the better part of a decade, and the WH-1000XM6 shows the company isn't letting up. The noise cancellation has been improved yet again—the adaptive algorithm reacts faster to sudden changes in ambient noise, and the seal around the ear has been redesigned for better passive isolation. LDAC and DSEE Ultimate upscaling deliver hi-res wireless audio, Sony has shaved weight compared to the XM5 for better long-session comfort, battery life holds strong at 30-plus hours with ANC on, and multipoint connectivity lets you stay paired to your laptop and phone simultaneously. Call quality has also seen a meaningful bump, which was one of the few genuine criticisms of previous generations.Philips S1209 won both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice awards at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026.The portable speaker market is dominated by names like JBL and Sony, which makes the Philips S1209's sweep here all the more impressive. This is a compact, easy-to-carry speaker that doesn't try to be a Bluetooth boombox—instead, it focuses on getting the basics right. The sound is clear and well-balanced for its size, with enough bass to fill a small room or a picnic setup without distortion, the battery lasts a full day out, and there's no gimmicky app ecosystem or feature overload. In a category where big-name brands tend to dominate on marketing alone, the Philips S1209 won because it simply sounds better than most at its price.Sony Bravia Theatre System 6 won both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice awards at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026.Sony's Bravia Theatre System 6 is what you get when a company decides to make a soundbar system that can genuinely replace a full home theatre setup. The system combines a soundbar with satellite speakers and a subwoofer that delivers deep, room-shaking bass, with Dolby Atmos and DTS:X support for spatial audio and excellent dialogue clarity—no more cranking up the volume just to hear actors mumble through a plot-critical scene. It integrates smoothly with Sony Bravia TVs through acoustic centre sync, essentially turning your TV's speakers and the soundbar into one unified audio system, and the whole setup looks clean in a living room without cables running across the ceiling.Samsung Neo QLED QN90F won both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice awards at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026.Samsung's Neo QLED lineup has been setting the standard for Mini LED TVs, and the QN90F is the crown jewel for 2025. The Quantum Matrix technology packs thousands of tiny LEDs behind the display for incredibly precise backlight control—bright scenes pop without washing out the dark parts, and peak brightness is among the highest in any consumer TV, making it ideal for well-lit rooms where OLEDs can struggle. The Neural Quantum Processor handles upscaling and AI-driven picture adjustments in real time, gaming performance is strong with a 144Hz refresh rate, VRR support, and sub-10ms input lag in Game Mode, and the anti-reflective coating cuts down on glare significantly. For the money, you're not finding a more well-rounded 4K TV than this.Xiaomi QLED TV FX Pro won both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice awards at the Gadgets Now Awards 2026.Xiaomi has been quietly dominating the affordable TV space in India, and the QLED TV FX Pro is the strongest example of why. You get a QLED panel with wide colour gamut, Dolby Vision and HDR10+ support, and a metal-frame design that looks far more expensive than it actually is. Brightness levels are solid for an affordable panel, PatchWall and Android TV run side by side giving you access to every streaming app you'd need, and the built-in speakers are passable for casual viewing—though you'll still want a soundbar for anything serious. For budget-conscious buyers who want QLED picture quality without the Samsung or LG premium, this is the one.
Article Summary: The Times of India-Gadgets Now Awards 2026 celebrated top audio and TV innovations. JBL Tour Pro 3 and Apple AirPods Pro 3 led TWS, while Sony WH-1000XM6 dominated headphones. Samsung's Neo QLED QN90F impressed in premium TVs, and Xiaomi's QLED TV FX Pro offered exceptional value in the affordable segment.

Laptops, smartwatches, cameras and home appliances took centre stage as the Times of India–Gadgets Now Awards 2025 wrapped up its remaining categories, rounding off a ceremony that honoured the most notable tech products of the past year. Held on Monday, February 23, in New Delhi, the awards spanned both the Editor's Choice and People's Choice segments, with voters and editors at times landing on very different picks. Samsung 's Galaxy Book 5 Pro stood out as a double People's Choice winner, claiming both Best Laptop and Best AI-Powered Gadget of the Year, while the Editor's Choice panel spread its recognition across a wider range of brands, including HP, Lenovo, Google, Dyson and Nikon.In the Editor's Choice laptop category, the HP Omnibook Flip X was named Best Laptop of the Year, while the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i took home the award for Best Gaming Laptop of the Year. In the wearables segment, the Google Pixel Watch 4 won Best Smartwatch of the Year, and the Boat Enigma Radiant was recognised as the Best Affordable Smartwatch of the Year.The Samsung Galaxy Book 5 Pro was named Best AI-Powered Gadget of the Year, while the Dyson Hushjet was recognised for Best Gadget with an Out-of-the-Box Design.In the home appliances segment, the Nuuk Ren Pro won the Best Vacuum Cleaner of the Year award. For imaging, the Nikon ZR was awarded Best Camera of the Year.In the People's Choice awards, the Samsung Galaxy Book 5 Pro claimed Best Laptop of the Year, with users also voting it as the Best AI-Powered Gadget of the Year, making it a double winner in the People's Choice segment. The Alienware 18 Area-51 was named Best Gaming Laptop of the Year.In wearables, the Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 Classic won Best Smartwatch of the Year, while the Noise ColorFit Pro 6 was recognised as the Best Affordable Smartwatch of the Year. The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge, which also featured in the smartphone categories, won Best Gadget with an Out-of-the-Box Design. The Eureka Forbes SmartClean Auto Bin S2 was voted Best Vacuum Cleaner of the Year, and the Canon R50V won Best Camera of the Year.Overall, the Times of India–Gadgets Now Awards 2025 recognised tech and appliance leaders, including Samsung, HP, Lenovo, Google, Alienware, Dyson, Nikon, Canon, Noise and Boat for their standout devices of the year.

Samsung's Galaxy Book 5 Pro stood out as a double People'schoice winner, claiming both Best Laptop and Best AI-Powered Gadget of the Year.

The Times of India Gadgets Now Awards 2025 brought together India's top tech innovations and celebrated the year's best gadgets in a star-studded ceremony that merged technology with glamour. The evening attracted a diverse mix of luminaries — from fashion designers and celebrity chefs to marketing leaders and legal experts — all coming together to honour excellence in consumer technology across multiple categories, including smartphones, smartwatches, audio devices and TVs.The evening opened with author Sutupa Basu and actor-entrepreneur Manya Pathak presenting awards for the year's best audio devices. JBL Tour Pro 3 claimed the Editor's Choice for Best TWS of the Year, while Apple AirPods Pro 3 won the People’s Choice. OnePlus dominated the affordable segment, with Nord Buds 3r winning both categories for Best Affordable TWS, while OnePlus Buds 4 took Editor's Choice for Best Affordable Premium TWS.Fashion and interior designer Varija Bajaj joined Panasonic executives Abhishek Verma and Dhiraj Sharma to present smartwatch honors.Google Pixel Watch 4 won Editor's Choice for Best Smartwatch, while Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 Classic secured People's Choice. In the affordable segment, Noise ColorFit Pro 6 took People's Choice over Boat Enigma Radiant's Editor's Choice win.Computing excellence was recognized with HP OmniBook Flip X winning Editor's Choice for Best Laptop of the Year, while Samsung Galaxy Book 5 Pro claimed People's Choice. For gaming laptops, dominated Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Editor’s Choice while Alienware 18 Area-51 won hearts in People’s Choice.Partner at JSA Law Venkatesh Raman Prasad and Sunanya Chibba presented awards for cutting-edge technology. Samsung Galaxy Book 5 Pro won Editor's Choice for Best AI-Powered Gadget, while Neosapien Neo 1 secured People's Choice. Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 Ultra swept both categories for Best Tablet of the Year.Celebrity chef Nishant Chaubey and Suraj Malik, CEO of Legacy Growth, recognized home audio winners, with Sony Bravia Theatre System 6 winning both Editor's and People's Choice awards.Global UX Head Deepika Dutta Kapoor and social entrepreneur Shalini Vig presented television honors. Samsung Neo QLED QN90F dominated the premium segment, winning both Editor's and People's Choice for Best TV of the Year. Xiaomi QLED TV FX Pro swept the affordable TV category in both voting streams.Home appliance excellence was recognized with Nuuk Ren Pro winning Editor's Choice for Best Vacuum Cleaner, while Eureka Forbes SmartClean Auto Bin S2 took People's Choice. In the standout design category, Dyson Hushjet Purifier Compact won Editor's Choice for Best Gadget with an Out-of-the-Box Design, while Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge captured People's Choice.Partner at JSA Law Vishnu Sudarsan and Dr. Jai Madan presented photography awards, with Nikon ZR winning Editor's Choice for Best Camera of the Year and Canon R50V claiming People's Choice.The smartphone categories proved highly competitive across all segments. Indian actor Rahul Dev and Ramakrishna P, CEO of India Mobile Congress, presented affordable and gaming smartphone awards. Nothing Phone 3(a) won Editor's Choice for Best Affordable Smartphone while Samsung Galaxy A17 secured People's Choice. In gaming, Vivo T4 Ultra took Editor's Choice while OnePlus 15R won People's Choice.OnePlus 13s claimed Editor's Choice for Best Affordable Premium Smartphone, with Google Pixel 9A winning People's Choice in the same category.The evening's most prestigious smartphone honors were presented by Kapila Sethi, Director of Marketing at William Grant & Sons, and humanitarian Varun Pruthi. Samsung dominated the premium categories, with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 sweeping both Editor's and People's Choice for Best Foldable Smartphone.In the flagship segment, Apple iPhone 17 won Editor's Choice for Best Premium Smartphone, while Samsung Galaxy S25 captured People's Choice. For camera excellence, Google Pixel 10 Pro XL won Editor's Choice while Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra secured People's Choice.The night’s ultimate honor — Best Smartphone of the Year — went to Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra in both Editor's and People's Choice categories, cementing Samsung's position as the mobile industry's gold standard.
Article Summary: The Times of India Gadgets Now Awards 2025 brought together India's top tech innovations and celebrated the year's best gadgets in a star-studded ceremony that merged technology with glamour. The evening attracted a diverse mix of luminaries — from fashion designers and celebrity chefs to marketing leaders and legal experts — all coming together to honour excellence in consumer technology.

Article Title: Romance Scam : Online love or online trap? How romance scam can steal your hearts and money, spot the red flags, and stay safe online
Article Text:
AI Generated

What are romance scams: The first signs you might miss



Warning signs you might be targeted



If someone quickly professes love, it might be a warning. If they avoid video calls or give endless excuses for not meeting in person. If they push to move conversations off the dating platform to WhatsApp, Telegram, or email, that’s another.

Requesting money: Especially cryptocurrency, gift cards, or wire transfers, that’s a major one to look upon.

Experts also point out combative behaviour when questioned, inconsistent stories, or minimal online presence as key indicators.

Even grammar mistakes can be a clue, particularly if they claim to be fluent in English.

And sometimes, banks will alert you if suspicious activity is detected. It might feel awkward. Embarrassing even. But it’s a red flag worth heeding.

How scammers collect money



How to stay safe from romance scams online



Go slowly and ask questions.

Observe inconsistencies.

Keep conversations on the dating platform at first and limit what you share online.

Reverse image searches for photos can help and always trust your gut.

If something feels off, it probably is.

Talk to someone you trust about your online connections.

Report suspicious behaviour to the platform.

Notify law enforcement if needed.

The Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) Tip Line reportedly helps people report potential romance scams and stay anonymous if they want.

Online dating can feel like a lifeline, meeting new people, swapping stories, maybe even feeling sparks fly. But it seems the digital world isn’t always hearts and roses. Romance scams are on the rise. They target people of all ages, but older adults, especially those who are recently widowed or divorced, appear particularly vulnerable. Scammers reportedly know that loneliness can make people more trusting and that having savings makes them a bigger target. Romance scams prey on emotions more than money. They might appear charming, caring, or attentive. But experts say vigilance, research, and slow decision-making are crucial.As reported, Beatrice, in her late 60s, met a man online during the COVID-19 pandemic. He wasn’t George Clooney, but he resembled her late husband, who had died months earlier. It seemed comforting. Someone to talk to, to fill the quiet hours. Until things started to feel off.It often begins slowly and starts with friendly chats at first, small talk, compliments. Scammers might claim they work abroad or have a high-status, glamorous job.They send photos, sometimes fake, AI-generated, sometimes stolen from other people. Their goal is totally on to gain your trust and make you feel special.It seems minor at first. But then they start dropping hints of love or affection unusually quickly. Weeks instead of months. Suddenly, you feel emotionally invested. And that’s when the financial requests begin, which sometimes start small and gradually grow shockingly large.Older adults are often the main targets. The Federal Trade Commission reported nearly 70,000 victims of romance scams in 2022 alone, with almost $240 million lost. That’s reportedly a big increase from previous years.There are subtle red flags:Sometimes they prefer payment methods that are hard to reverse. Cryptocurrency and bank wires top the list, gift cards come next. Payment apps like PayPal or Venmo are used less often. The common factor: once the money is sent, it’s nearly impossible to get it back, leaving victims with limited recovery options and significant emotional stress.Experts say falling victim isn’t a sign of weakness. Scammers are highly manipulative, often working in organised groups with pre-written scripts. They know how to exploit emotions, particularly loneliness and grief, building trust over time before gradually introducing requests for money.
Article Summary: Experts say falling victim isn’t a sign of weakness. Scammers are highly manipulative, often working in organised groups with pre-written scripts. They know how to exploit emotions, particularly loneliness and grief, building trust over time. Romance scams are on the rise and target people of all ages.

Article Title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro may see the return of this iconic colour
Article Text:
Too close on the colour wheel



Apple iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max expected launch



New A20 Pro chip may power the iPhone 18 Pro Max



Apple is gearing up for the launch of its new devices next week. However, a new leak about the iPhone 18 launch has now surfaced online. According to a report by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman Apple is experimenting with a deep red colour option for the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max. This move of the company is followed by Cosmic Orange variant on the iPhone 17 Pro. For those unaware, the Cosmic Orange colour of iPhone 17 Pro Max gained a lot of popularity in China, where the customers linked it to Hermès’ signature orange. Gurman notes that red is currently the flagship colour in testing, though Apple may keep orange in the lineup as well.“Apple is now considering a deep red color for its next lineup. Given the success of orange, I wouldn’t be surprised if the company keeps that option around and just adds the red as an additional choice. But red and orange might be a little too close on the color wheel to have both,” reads the Bloomberg report.While orange helped drive sales, Apple faces a design decision: whether to keep both orange and red, which sit close together on the colour spectrum. Gurman suggests that rumoured purple and brown options may simply be variations of the red shade under consideration.Gurman has also said that Apple’s long-rumoured foldable iPhone, Gurman reports that the company plans to avoid bold colours altogether. Instead, the foldable is expected to launch in more utilitarian tones reminiscent of early iPhone models — likely dark gray/black and light silver/white.Apple generally announces new iPhone models at its Fall event in September. This is rumoured to change in 2026. According to reports, iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max in the typical September timeframe. However, the standard iPhone 18 may be skipped at the September event this year. As per reports, it may be unveiled next year in March along with iPhone Air 2.Apple's new A20 Pro chip, which will be the company's first processor manufactured using a 2nm process, is expected to power the iPhone 18 Pro Max. Additionally, the chip will employ "Wafer-Level Multi-Chip Module" (WMCM) packaging, making it more compact, powerful, and energy-efficient than earlier iterations.Moreover, Apple may switch from third-party chips to its own modem for the first time in the Pro models. This modification should greatly extend battery life, especially when paired with the A20 Pro's powerful processor.
Article Summary: A new leak about the iPhone 18 launch has now surfaced online. According to a report by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman Apple is experimenting with a deep red colour option for the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max. This move of the company is followed by Cosmic Orange variant on the iPhone 17 Pro. Gurman notes that red is currently the flagship colour in testing.
